purpose: Identifies the negatively skewed payoff profile where fiscal deficits simultaneously compress credit spreads (rewarding carry) and increase fiscal dominance risk (increasing tail risk).
components: Kalecki-Levy identity: deficits → corporate profits → credit spread compression
Fiscal dominance risk: same deficits → debt sustainability deterioration → tail risk
Payoff profile: carry narrows as underlying risk rises
Regime-conditional factor allocation: shift from carry-seeking to tail-hedging as fiscal dominance signals activate
current_assessment: The carry trap is currently operating — credit spreads are compressed while fiscal fundamentals deteriorate. The deficit is the common cause of both phenomena, creating a feedback loop that rewards complacency.
limitations: No precise threshold for when the trap becomes a measurably bad risk-reward
Requires regime identification, which is itself subject to the small-n problem
Harvey-Liu-Zhu framework shows fiscal dominance beta would fail t-stat threshold of ~3.0 in cross-sectional tests
Timing the regime shift is not possible with available tools
